design_plan: Cinematic editorial competitor page with a dense CSS-grid bento layout, GSAP-enhanced but guarded motion, Geist/Satoshi font stack, dark premium palette, and AIDA flow. Hero uses split headline + ambient motion. Bento sections explicitly use grid-auto-flow: dense to avoid gaps. Clear contrast CTAs. Animations degrade gracefully if GSAP CDN fails.
headline: Super vs Orchids — Personal AI Agents for Real Computer Work

site_brief.goal: Create a fair but persuasive competitor comparison page for Super vs Orchids. Use supplied sources for current facts, avoid fake claims, and make the practical argument that Super is better suited for repeated computer-use work because its cache can lower repeated execution cost.
site_brief.title: Super vs Orchids

summary: A fair, editorial comparison of Super and Orchids for people choosing a personal AI agent. Orchids focuses on messaging‑centric and consumer experiences, while Super is built for repeated computer‑use workflows, with agents that operate full desktops and reuse a computer-use cache to reduce repeated execution cost. We also place both in context alongside Folk, Siri, ChatGPT, Gemini, and Grok as the personal agent market accelerates.
